sutta: the buddha said monks these 4 are worthy of a stupa what for a tatargata arahant a fully enlightened 1 is worthy of a stupa a pacheco buddha a tattagata's disciple and a rajah who rolls the wheel are worthy of a stupa these are the 4 that's the end of the suta
commentary: so here what i mean worthy of a stupa the buddha said that if a person is worthy of a stupa when he passes away and the corpse is cremated then you take the ashes from after the cremation and you put them into a stupa and you venerate the ashes you pay respect to the ashes and who are these 4 people who are worthy of being venerated the first 1 is a samasan 1 who is enlightened and teaches the dhamma sets rolling the dhamma wheel second 1 is the pachika buddha pachika buddha you find is also worthy of veneration although some later books say that the pachika buddha is selfish etc this is not what the buddha says because yeah you find that buddha patika buddha is also worthy of veneration because teaching is not only by words actions as they say actions speak louder than words so if a person like apache buddha sets a very good example as a very holy man it impresses a lot of people that itself is a teaching to many people third 1 is the tatar gathers disciple meaning savaka savaka here implies the 8 aryans the 8 types of aryans the 4 fruits and the 4 path attainers and you'll find later in the sutasa that the buddha talks about these areas as 8 persons not as 4 persons 8 types of persons and all of them are worthy of veneration and then the last 1 is a wheel turning king a wheel turning king is 1 who is so uh so so good so virtuous and he rules with dhamma as his guide so that the whole world wants him to be the king so he looks he he is a person with a lot of what they call blessings paramisa you'll notice here in our theravada sutas these earlier sutas there's no mention of bodhisattva or bodhisattva which are found in the mahayana books mahayana books talk a lot about the bodhisattvas or bodhisattvas whereas in our theravada books they are not even mentioned here as being worthy of veneration in the stupa [Music] being 1 who is not yet enlightened 1 who is not yet an arahant or a buddha it's just walking the path to become to become enlightened so like all of us can be considered buddhist to us because we are on the path learning the dhamma trying to get out of samsara trying to end the round of rebirths that is giving us much pain so we come to the next suta
